draft.introduction: Mark’s crucifixion account is the starkest of the four Gospels. Jesus speaks only once from the cross—the cry of dereliction from Psalm 22: “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”

translation.text: And when the sixth hour had come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour. And at the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, “Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?” And the curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom. And when the centurion saw that in this way he breathed his last, he said, “Truly this man was the Son of God!”
translation.original_text: ελωι ελωι λεμα σαβαχθανι ... ἀληθῶς οὗτος ὁ ἄνθρωπος υἱὸς θεοῦ ἦν

translator_notes.note: The centurion’s confession—“Truly this man was the Son of God” (υἱὸς θεοῦ)—is the climax of Mark’s Gospel. A pagan soldier sees what the disciples could not: Jesus’ identity is revealed not through miracles but through suffering and death.
